title: Lux Trading Firm Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026)

meta_description: Lux Trading Firm Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026). Lux Trading Firm (4.1★, $99/mo). Step-by-step setup, features walkthrough, and pro tips. Updated
excerpt: Complete guide to setting up and using Lux Trading Firm — from account creation to pro-level tips.
content:
What is Lux Trading Firm?

Lux Trading Firm is a London-based proprietary trading firm that provides funded trading accounts to experienced traders, with account sizes ranging from $10,000 to $2.5 million. Founded in 2020, the firm specializes in offering a streamlined 1-step evaluation process, allowing traders to access capital quickly without lengthy multi-phase assessments. The platform supports forex, indices, commodities, metals, and crypto markets, with profit splits reaching up to 80% and transparent scaling plans that reward consistent performance. Rated 4.1 out of 5 stars, Lux Trading Firm appeals to experienced forex traders, algorithmic traders using Expert Advisors, and multi-asset traders seeking flexible capital and favorable trading conditions.

How to Create Your Lux Trading Firm Account

Getting started with Lux Trading Firm involves a straightforward registration process designed to minimize friction. Here's what to expect:

  • Visit the Registration Page — Navigate to the Lux Trading Firm website and click "Create Account." You'll need a valid email address, which becomes your login credential.
  • Enter Basic Information — Provide your full name, country of residence, phone number, and trading experience level. Lux Trading Firm requires you to confirm you have experience trading forex or other supported markets.
  • Email Verification — Check your email for a verification link. Click it within 24 hours to activate your account. This typically takes less than 5 minutes.
  • Choose Your Evaluation Tier — Select from Starter ($10K), Standard ($25K), Advanced ($50K), or Professional ($100K) accounts. Each tier has a monthly fee ($99-$499) and includes access to the evaluation phase.
  • Complete Payment — Enter payment details for your monthly subscription. Lux Trading Firm accepts credit/debit cards and wire transfers. Your account activates immediately after payment clears.
  • Broker Connection — Lux Trading Firm provides MT4, MT5, and cTrader connection details. Download the platform and input the server credentials supplied during signup.

Verification Requirements: You'll need proof of identity (passport or driver's license), proof of address (utility bill dated within 3 months), and trading history documentation if claimed. Most traders complete full verification within 48 hours.

Estimated Setup Time: 15-20 minutes for initial registration, plus 2-3 days for broker connection and verification completion.

Setting Up Lux Trading Firm for the First Time

Once your account is activated, configure your trading environment to maximize performance. The Lux Trading Firm dashboard presents your account status, live balance, open positions, and historical performance analytics in one view.

Dashboard Layout: The main dashboard displays your funded account balance, current drawdown (critical for risk management), and a live P&L counter. Below this, you'll see your trading history sorted by trade date, entry price, exit price, and profit/loss. The sidebar contains shortcuts to your account settings, evaluation progress, and withdrawal requests.

Broker Connection Setup: After payment, Lux Trading Firm emails you a unique server address and login credentials for MT4, MT5, or cTrader (depending on your choice). Open your chosen platform, add a new account, input the server details exactly as provided, and enter your login credentials. Complete the connection within 24 hours to avoid account suspension. If using cTrader, enable two-factor authentication for enhanced security.

Key Settings to Configure: Navigate to Account Settings and set your notification preferences (email alerts for drawdown warnings, position limits exceeded, and withdrawal approvals). Enable the "Risk Monitor" feature, which automatically alerts you when you approach the maximum daily loss limit (usually 5-10% of funded capital). Disable "Trailing Stop" rules if you want to hold overnight positions — Lux Trading Firm allows this, but you must manually enable it per account.

Performance Analytics Setup: Access the Performance Analytics tab to track daily, weekly, and monthly returns. Set your benchmark (many traders use the S&P 500 or commodity index for comparison). This data feeds into your scaling evaluation, so accurate setup ensures you see progress toward larger account tiers.

Essential Features You Should Know

1. Broker Integration (MT4/MT5/cTrader Support)

Lux Trading Firm integrates directly with three major trading platforms. MT4 suits forex and commodity traders, MT5 adds indices and crypto capabilities, and cTrader is ideal for scalpers wanting low latency. Switching between platforms mid-month is allowed — simply disconnect from one server and add the new one. Your balance and P&L sync automatically across all active connections, so you can trade forex on MT5 while monitoring commodities on MT4.

2. Automated Trading (Expert Advisors Allowed)

Unlike many prop firms that restrict EAs, Lux Trading Firm welcomes Expert Advisors without penalty. If you run an EA-based strategy, upload your .ex4 or .ex5 file to the account management portal, and the firm pre-approves it if it complies with their risk rules (no martingale systems, maximum leverage capped at 100:1). Your EA can run 24/5 on Lux Trading Firm's dedicated VPS, which executes orders with sub-100ms latency.

3. Performance Analytics

The analytics dashboard shows daily/weekly/monthly win rates, average profit per trade, profit factor, and Sharpe ratio. Use these metrics to identify which market conditions favor your strategy. The system flags "red flag patterns" — like a 50%+ win rate with negative expectancy — so you can adjust. Export raw trade data as CSV to backtest in your preferred platform.

4. Risk Management Tools

Lux Trading Firm enforces a daily loss limit (typically 5% of funded capital) and a monthly loss limit (10-15%). The Risk Monitor widget shows your current usage in real-time. Once you hit the daily limit, the account auto-locks until the next trading day. The firm also caps maximum position size to prevent over-leveraging — for a $25K account, the limit is typically 2.5 standard lots on forex.

5. Education Resources

Access a library of video tutorials (setup, strategy tips, risk management fundamentals), weekly webinars hosted by successful Lux funded traders, and a private Discord community. The webinars often feature live trade reviews and Q&A sessions. New traders benefit most from the "EA Configuration" and "News Trading Rules" guides, both specific to Lux requirements.

6. Scaling Plan

Pass the evaluation (3 months of consistent profit without violating drawdown rules), and your account automatically scales. A $25K account becoming profitable scales to $50K within 30 days. Hit profit targets again, and it scales to $100K, then $250K, up to $2.5M. Scaling requires no additional fees or re-evaluation — just continued compliance.

7. Bi-Weekly Payouts

Once you're a funded trader, payouts occur every two weeks (typically Wednesdays). Request a withdrawal up to 80% of your profits via the Withdrawals tab. Lux processes transfers within 3-5 business days to your bank account. No hidden fees or minimum withdrawal thresholds.

Lux Trading Firm Pricing: Which Plan Should You Choose?

Starter Plan: $10K Account + $99/Month

Best for traders new to prop firm evaluation or those testing a strategy with real capital. The $10K account forces disciplined position sizing and reveals whether your strategy scales. The monthly cost ($99) is negligible if profitable, but accumulates if you fail the evaluation and restart. Most traders spend $300-$600 before passing evaluation.

Standard Plan: $25K Account + $199/Month

The most popular tier among intermediate forex and commodity traders. A $25K account allows position sizes large enough to generate meaningful profits (5-10% monthly target translates to $1,250-$2,500) while keeping leverage and risk reasonable. Monthly fees ($199) are quickly recouped in the evaluation phase if your strategy is profitable. This tier suits traders with proven backtests seeking their first funded account.

Advanced Plan: $50K Account + $299/Month

Ideal for traders with consistent 3-6 month profit track records or experienced algorithmic traders running multiple EAs. Larger position sizes ($5K-$10K per trade) suit complex multi-timeframe strategies and portfolio approaches. Higher monthly fees ($299) demand higher profit targets to justify, so reserve this tier for traders with documented edge.

Professional Plan: $100K Account + $499/Month

Designed for traders scaling into serious capital. This tier suits hedge fund managers, prop desk veterans, or teams pooling resources. A $100K account with 80% profit split generates $4,000-$8,000 monthly profit for a 4-8% return. The $499 monthly fee is trivial at this capital level.

Verdict: Start with the Starter or Standard tier unless you have a track record proving consistent profitability. The $99-$199 monthly fee is a small cost to validate your edge under real market conditions.

Pro Tips for Getting the Most Out of Lux Trading Firm

1. Test Your Strategy in Demo First, Then Commit One Full Month

Before joining Lux Trading Firm, paper trade your strategy using the same broker and account size. Once enrolled, focus entirely on execution for 30 days without changing your system. Lux's 1-step evaluation removes the pressure of multi-phase tests, so dedicate one month to proving consistency rather than trying to "game" the evaluation with outsized trades.

2. Leverage the News Trading Permission Advantage

Unlike competitors like FTMO, Lux Trading Firm explicitly allows news trading and holding positions through high-impact economic events (NFP, ECB decisions, Fed announcements). If your strategy thrives during volatility spikes, Lux offers an edge. Document which news events align with your strategy and concentrate your largest positions during those windows.

3. Use the Scaling Plan to Compound Gains Mathematically

Your account automatically scales from $25K → $50K → $100K if profitable. A 5% monthly return on $25K generates $1,250; on $100K, that same return generates $5,000. Plan your first 6 months to hit scaling milestones ($2,500 net profit per month) so the account grows without additional fees or re-evaluation. Track scaling triggers in a spreadsheet.

4. Connect an EA and Let It Run 24/5 on the Lux VPS

If you've coded an EA (or licensed one), upload it to Lux and let it execute autonomously. The firm's VPS ensures zero requotes and low-latency fills. This generates passive income during sleep or while you trade manually. The EA's performance directly qualifies you for scaling, so optimize it monthly using the exported trade data.

5. Monitor the Risk Monitor Widget Daily, Not Weekly

Lux caps daily drawdown at 5%. A single 6% losing day locks your account. Check the Risk Monitor widget every morning and before large position entries. If you're close to the daily limit (3-4%), avoid high-impact trades that day. This discipline prevents emotional over-trading and preserves your monthly loss budget.

6. Compare Your Performance Against Lux's Benchmark Monthly

The Performance Analytics tab includes a default S&P 500 benchmark. If your monthly return trails the S&P by 300+ bps (basis points), reconsider your strategy or market conditions. A 5% monthly return beats most passive indexes, but if you're generating 2%, the evaluation fee eats returns. Adjust leverage or timeframe bias accordingly.

7. Join the Discord Community and Replicate Winning Strategies

Lux's private Discord community features daily trade setups, after-hours analysis, and peer strategies that passed evaluation. Rather than isolating yourself, engage with profitable traders. Many share non-proprietary entry signals or risk management frameworks that accelerate your learning curve.

Common Lux Trading Firm Issues and How to Fix Them

Issue 1: "Account Locked Due to Daily Drawdown Limit"

This happens when losses exceed 5% of funded capital in a single day. The account auto-locks until the next trading session opens. Solution: Check the Risk Monitor before each trade to avoid surprises. If you know a high-impact news event is coming (FOMC, NFP), reduce position sizes to 50% of normal. The unlock is automatic — no customer service ticket required.

Issue 2: "EA Not Uploading to Account Portal"

The most common cause is file format mismatch. Lux accepts .ex4 (MT4) and .ex5 (MT5) files only; no .mq4 or .mq5 source code. Solution: Compile your EA in MetaEditor under the correct format, then upload the compiled binary. If it still fails, check that your EA has no martingale logic or hardcoded leverage above 100:1 — Lux auto-rejects these. Test the EA in the demo environment first to verify it's running.

Issue 3: "Profit Split Lower Than 80% in Withdrawal Statement"

Some traders misinterpret the profit split calculation. Lux takes 20% of your realized profits before payout. Solution: Calculate it manually. If you earned $10,000 profit in a month, your payout is $8,000 (80% of $10,000). This is deducted from the next withdrawal. The monthly $99-$499 subscription fee is separate and is charged whether you profit or lose.

Issue 4: "Evaluation Failed After 2 Months — Can I Restart?"

If you violate the monthly drawdown limit (typically 10%) or breach risk rules, the evaluation ends. Solution: Pay the subscription fee again ($99-$499 depending on tier) to restart with a fresh account. The previous account is archived for historical review. Most traders succeed on their second or third attempt after adjusting position sizing or strategy parameters based on the failed attempt's data.

Is Lux Trading Firm Worth It? Our Verdict

Lux Trading Firm delivers on its promise: fast capital access, favorable profit splits (80%), and actual scalability up to $2.5M without repeated re-evaluations. The 4.1/5 rating reflects consistent trader satisfaction, and the 1-step evaluation structure stands out against competitors like Topstep (which require 3 phases). For experienced forex and commodity traders with documented strategy edge, the $99-$299 monthly fee pays for itself within the first profitable month. The explicit allowance for news trading, Expert Advisors, and overnight holds gives traders the flexibility missing at older firms.

However, newer traders should proceed cautiously. Founded in 2020, Lux lacks the 15+ year track record of FTMO, and evaluation fees are non-refundable — budget $300-$600 for restarts if your first attempt fails. The lack of a free demo evaluation means you're paying to test your strategy, not practicing.

Choose Lux Trading Firm if: You're a profitable forex or commodity trader seeking $25K-$100K in funded capital with 80% profit splits, you run Expert Advisors, or you need flexibility with overnight positions and news trading. Look elsewhere if: You're a complete beginner (paper trade first for 6 months), you need a trial evaluation before paying, or you're risk-averse and want a 20-year-old established firm.
